km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury是Sir Adam de Bury的兒子，於1082年出生在英國。他至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury是Sir Adam de Bury的儿子，于1082年出生在英国。他至少有1个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Buri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Markus, Kurt.
Swiss German and South German: from an Alemannic pet form of the personal name Burkhard (see Burkhart ).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Buro  .

variants.name: Burgh
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury was born in 1082, in England as the son of Sir Adam de Bury. He had at least 1 son. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury est né en 1082, à Angleterre, fils de Sir Adam de Bury. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury wurde 1082 in England, als Sohn von Sir Adam de Bury geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury è nato il nel 1082 nel England come figlio di Sir Adam de Bury. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James de Buryは1082年にイギリスでSir Adam de Buryの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury(은)는 1082년 영국에서 Sir Adam de Bury의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury nasceu em 1082, em Inglaterra filho de Sir Adam de Bury. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury родился в 1082 г. в местности Англия. Его отца звали Sir Adam de Bury. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Sir James de Bury nació en 1082, en Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Adam de Bury. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo.
